codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses all 3 original blocking/non-blocking issues. (1) localToUTC() in dashboard-client.tsx is simplified to `new Date(dateStr+'T'+timeStr).toISOString()`, eliminating the double-offset bug. (2) dayBoundsUTC() in todo-board.tsx and demo/todos/page.tsx receives the same fix. (3) getUserTimezone() is extracted to web/lib/timezone.ts and all 4 previously-duplicated copies are replaced with imports. The worker also proactively fixed the same double-offset bug in datetimeLocalToISO() in todo-card.tsx. TypeScript typecheck and Next.js build both pass. The commit is clean, atomic, and well-documented.

issues.description: Extra blank line left after removing the getUserTimezone() function definition. Two consecutive blank lines remain (cosmetic only).

The `_timezone` parameter is kept but unused in localToUTC(). While the underscore prefix correctly signals intentional non-use, the parameter could be removed entirely since all callers are in the same codebase and were updated in this commit. Same applies to dayBoundsUTC() in todo-board.tsx and demo/todos/page.tsx. Keeping it is acceptable for backward compatibility but adds minor API confusion.

No unit tests were added for the shared timezone utility. The worker noted this was verified via typecheck and build only. While acceptable for a bug fix, a simple unit test asserting that getUserTimezone() returns a valid IANA timezone string would improve confidence for future changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill referenced by the feature (skillName: 'frontend-worker') still does not exist. Worker noted in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'frontend-worker skill was not found/available - had to proceed without it'. This is the third consecutive feature flagging this gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: ['frontend-worker skill was not found/available - had to proceed without it']. Prior reviews for web-home-page and web-todos-page flagged the same issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The JavaScript Date parsing behavior (`new Date('YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss')` without Z suffix parses as browser-local time, with Z suffix parses as UTC) caused a bug that was replicated in 4 different files across 2 features by different workers. This JS gotcha should be documented in .factory/library/ to prevent recurrence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The same double-offset bug appeared in localToUTC (dashboard-client.tsx), dayBoundsUTC (todo-board.tsx, demo/todos/page.tsx), and datetimeLocalToISO (todo-card.tsx). All used `new Date(str)` (local parse) then added a UTC offset, doubling the shift.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix establishes web/lib/timezone.ts as the canonical location for shared browser-side timezone utilities. AGENTS.md could document that shared web utilities belong in web/lib/ to prevent future duplication.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Prior to this fix, getUserTimezone() was duplicated in 4 files. The fix consolidated to web/lib/timezone.ts and all components now import from there.

summary: PASS. The fix adequately addresses all original failures: (1) localToUTC() double-offset bug in dashboard-client.tsx is fixed by removing redundant offset calculation, (2) dayBoundsUTC() identical bug in todo-board.tsx and demo page is fixed the same way, (3) getUserTimezone() duplication across 4 files is resolved via shared web/lib/timezone.ts. The worker also proactively fixed the same bug in datetimeLocalToISO() in todo-card.tsx. All fixes use the correct approach: since browser TZ equals target TZ, `new Date('YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss').toISOString()` directly produces the correct UTC value. TypeScript typecheck and Next.js build pass. Only non-blocking cosmetic issues remain (extra blank line, unused _timezone parameters, no unit tests for the utility).
